title: Cronos Group Q3 2023 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Cronos reported Q3 2023 consolidated net revenue of $24.8 million, representing 22% year-over-year increase and 30% sequential growth. CEO highlighted we grew revenue by 22% year over year and 30% sequentially to $24.8 million. The company achieved adjusted gross margin of approximately 19%, excluding $0.7 million inventory write-down, marking 260 basis point sequential improvement. Spinach brand achieved number one overall brand in Canada as of October, number one in edibles with 17.9% market share, risen to number one in flower from number two, number three in vapes, and number seven in pre-rolls. Germany shipped first cannabis order to distributor Cansativa, targeting approximately 300,000 patients through 2,000 pharmacies. Australia planning November shipment to Vitura with 10% ownership stake; received $346,000 dividend in Q3. Israel maintained distribution despite war conditions; generated $5.7 million revenue. The company ended Q3 with approximately $840 million in cash with zero debt. Operating cash flow improved dramatically to negative $180,000 while free cash flow reached negative $510,000. Cronos on track to achieve $20-$25 million operating expense reduction target for 2023.
